replication_study.hypothesis: Participants assigned to the contingent-feedback condition will exhibit a higher average one-dimensional (1D) accuracy score at the test phase than participants assigned to the full-information condition.

data_plan.dataset_identifier: analysis-data.csv (processed Prolific replication of Rich & Gureckis 2018 Experiment 1)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single data-collection wave, Bee-classification task with contingent vs full-information feedback
data_plan.access_details: Dataset supplied in /app/data/original/24/input/replication_data/ ; no further restrictions.

qualification.explanation: Data were gathered with the same task logic and variable structure as the original study but from a new participant pool, fulfilling independent-sample replication requirements.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Variables ‘condition’, ‘correct_1d_a’, ‘correct_1d_b’, and ‘phase’ match the operationalisation described in the original methods (post_registration.json; analysis.Rmd reproduces the original scoring algorithm).  Stimulus coding and rule-classification thresholds exactly mirror the original published analysis (analysis.Rmd).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original data came from Amazon Mechanical Turk with N=101 (Exp-1) (post_registration.json), whereas the replication data use Prolific with N=474 and were collected later in time.  This provides a new, larger sample while holding task mechanics constant.

data_plan.notes: File already excludes subjects who reported using external aids; raw Prolific json files are also included but not required.  The data frame encodes booleans as TRUE/FALSE and categorical strings exactly as expected by the original R code.

planned_method.steps:
1. Load analysis-data.csv.
2. Restrict rows to phase == "Test".
3. For each participant compute 1D accuracy = max(mean(correct_1d_a), mean(correct_1d_b)).
4. Create two vectors of 1D accuracy by condition.
5. Conduct independent-samples t-test (equal variances) on the two vectors.
6. Report group means, SDs, t, df, p-value, and Cohen’s d.
7. Save participant-level summary CSV for transparency and produce histogram plot.
planned_method.models: Independent-samples t-test; effect size Cohen’s d
planned_method.outcome_variable: Participant-level 1D accuracy score during the test phase
planned_method.tools_software: R (tidyverse, stats, papaja/broom for APA output) ‑ implemented through analysis.Rmd or an equivalent R script.
planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion; rows with NA in correctness variables are already absent in dataset.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Reject H0 if p < .05 and direction (contingent > full-information) matches hypothesis.

files.analysis.Rmd: End-to-end RMarkdown notebook that loads raw Prolific json files, builds analysis-data.csv, computes learning-strategy metrics, performs t-test, and generates figures.
files.analysis-data.csv: Processed, tidy trial-level dataset used for replication analysis.
files.data_exp1.csv: Alternate raw export of experiment 1 (not used in focal test but available).
files.participant_test_summary.R (to be writte: Minimal R script that reproduces only the focal 1D-score analysis for automated runs.

codebase.notes: The existing RMarkdown is interactive; for automated pipelines we will extract the relevant R code into participant_test_summary.R.  Dependencies: tidyverse, jsonlite, papaja/broom.

analysis.instructions: Run Rscript participant_test_summary.R (or knit analysis.Rmd) inside the container; script reads /app/data/original/24/input/replication_data/analysis-data.csv and writes participant_test_summary.csv plus console output of test results.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Group means, mean difference, t-statistic, p-value, 95% CI, and Cohen’s d will be compared with the original reported CI [.03, .14] and descriptive means (.83 vs .75).
